CSCE 491 Syllabus

Section 211 (33674)

Research

Fall 2025 - College Station

Course Information

Meeting Location:  422 Peterson

Meeting Times:   TBD - weekly

The credit hours listed below come from Howdy, which is 0-4 hours. Typically,

students take this for 3 credit hours per semester. Please verify you are registered

for the correct number of hours.

Credit Hours:  0 TO 4

Instructor Details

Robert Lightfoot

Email:  Use Canvas inbox for course issues. Others,

rob.light@tamu.edu

Office:  422 Peterson

Phone:  979 845-2611

Office Hours

Posted in Canvas

Preferred Contact Method

Canvas inbox, WhatsApp

Webpage:

https://engineering.tamu.edu/cse/profiles/Lightfoot-

Robert.html

Biography

A. Robert Lightfoot earned his Ph.D. in

Multidisciplinary Engineering and B.S. in

Computer Science from Texas A&M

University, and an M.S. in Computer

Science from Southern Methodist

University.

B. His career includes work at Ericsson and

DSC (Motorola) and independent

development of smart sorting systems.

C. His teaching and research focus on

software engineering and computer

science education.

Socials

Study abroad and Fish camp are amazing!

Course Description

Research conducted under the direction of a faculty member in computer science.

May be taken four times for credit.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Junior or senior classification and approval of

instructor.

Special Course Designation

None

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Read and understand research in their field.

Propose and write a research paper in their field.

Present their work at a regional conference.

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Optional

No Textbook needed.

Grading Policy

CSCE Undergraduate Research – Grading Policy

Letter

Grade   Criteria

None

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Read and understand research in their field.

Propose and write a research paper in their field.

Present their work at a regional conference.

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Optional

No Textbook needed.

Grading Policy

CSCE Undergraduate Research – Grading Policy

Letter

Grade   Criteria

A

Successfully completes  all course requirements  on

time and to a high standard. Demonstrates consistent

engagement, meets research milestones, and

contributes meaningfully to the project.

B

Completes  90% or more  of assignments and milestones,

but may have minor delays or reduced depth in some

tasks. Engagement and contribution remain strong.

C

Completes  75–89%  of assignments/milestones. May

have multiple late or incomplete submissions, with

noticeable gaps in engagement or research contribution.

CSCE Undergraduate Research – Grading Policy

Letter

Grade   Criteria

D

Completes  60–74%  of assignments/milestones.

Frequently late or incomplete work, limited engagement

with the research project, and/or missed major

deliverables.

F

Completes  less than 60%  of assignments/milestones or

fails to meet essential course requirements. Lack of

engagement and minimal contribution to research.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

There are hard deadlines for the conferences and the URS program at

TAMU. We will abide by those dates.

Course Schedule

The schedule will be discussed in our weekly meetings.

Define research goals

Review current work

Write and submit an abstract

Write and submit a paper

Create presentation

Present work

Complete URS requirements

ASEE-GSW  Abstracts are usually due in November, the paper is usually

due in late December, and the conference is usually the first 2-3 days

of spring break.

URS  We will follow the due dates posted by URS.

Additional Course Information

AI Statement

We will use AI in various capacities in this course. Full disclosure is required.
